methods.description: This method describes the acquisition and processing procedures for multibeam bathymetry, backscatter, and high-resolution single-channel seismic data collected during research cruises.

steps.description: Geophysical data (bathymetry and backscatter) were acquired during the research cruise "MagicIgag0910" carried out on November 2009 onboard R/V Maria Grazia using Kongsberg EM 3002D (300 kHz, Kongsberg Maritime, Kongsberg. Norway).
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire multibeam bathymetry and backscatter data using a multibeam echosounder.

steps.description: Sparker-high-resolution single-channel profiles were acquired during the research cruise "Eleonora 2003" onboard R/V Urania.
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire high-resolution single-channel seismic profiles using a Sparker system.

steps.description: Bathymetric data were post-processed with the software Caris Hips and Sips 8.1.7 (Teledyne CARIS, Fredericton, NB Canada).
steps.searchable_summary: Post-process raw multibeam bathymetry data using specialized software.

steps.description: the backscatter was processed through the Geo-Coder tool.
steps.searchable_summary: Process raw backscatter data using a specific tool.

steps.description: High-resolution digital elevation model (DEM) was produced (cell size 2 m) in order to analyze the surface morphology.
steps.searchable_summary: Generate a high-resolution digital elevation model (DEM) from processed bathymetry.

steps.description: Backscatter mosaic with 1 m pixelresolution was produced to analyze the areal variability of seafloor sediments.
steps.searchable_summary: Generate a backscatter mosaic from processed backscatter data.

steps.description: High-resolution seismic data were processed and analyzed using GeoSuite AllWorks (Geo Marine Survey Systems, Rotterdam. The Netherlands) and Kingdom Suite software (version 8.8, IHS Markit, London. UK).
steps.searchable_summary: Process and analyze high-resolution seismic data using specialized software.

steps.description: These data [DEM] were used to analyze the surface morphology.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze surface morphology using the digital elevation model (DEM).

steps.description: These data [Backscatter mosaic] were used... to analyze the areal variability of seafloor sediments.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze the areal variability of seafloor sediments using the backscatter mosaic.

steps.description: These data [processed seismic data] were used to characterize the seismostratigraphic setting of the study area.
steps.searchable_summary: Characterize the seismostratigraphic setting using processed and analyzed seismic data.

methods.method_name: Sediment Core Age Model and Sedimentation Rate Calculation
methods.description: This method describes how age models were established for sediment cores using 14C AMS dating results and how sedimentation rates were calculated based on these age models.

steps.description: The 14 C AMS datings indicate that the sediments of core CS1 were deposited during the last~9600 yr BP, whereas the core Caro1 was deposited during the last 16,000 yr BP. The age model is provided by four and three 14 C AMS datings for CS1 and Caro1 cores, respectively (Table 1).
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain radiocarbon dating results (14C AMS) for sediment core samples at specific depths.
outputs: Table of depths and corresponding 14C AMS ages (yr BP) for each core

steps.description: The age model is provided by four and three 14 C AMS datings for CS1 and Caro1 cores, respectively (Table 1).
steps.searchable_summary: Construct an age-depth model for each sediment core based on 14C AMS dates.
inputs: Table of depths and 14C AMS ages
outputs: Age-depth model (function or table) for each core

steps.description: Assuming invariant sedimentation rates between two successive radiocarbon dates, it is possible to estimate the sedimentation rates along the cores...
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate sedimentation rates for intervals along each core using the age model, assuming invariant rates between dated points.
outputs: Sedimentation rates (cm/yr) for specified intervals in each core
